title: Joint Doctrine Ontology (JDO)
description: The Joint Doctrine Ontology provides a formal, machine-readable representation of joint military doctrine concepts and their relationships. JDO enables semantic interoperability across DoD systems by defining a shared vocabulary for joint operations, planning, and force management concepts drawn from Joint Publications (JPs). It supports automated reasoning about doctrine applicability and cross-domain concept alignment.
managingOrganization: Joint Staff J7 / Joint Warfighting Development

title: Web Ontology Language (OWL 2)
description: OWL 2 is a W3C Recommendation for creating ontologies on the Semantic Web. It provides a formal language for defining classes, properties, individuals, and axioms that enable machine reasoning over knowledge domains. OWL 2 is the foundational ontology language used across the DoD and IC for building domain-specific ontologies, including JDO, ISR ontologies, and data fabric semantic layers.

title: DAF Data Fabric Ontology
description: An internal ontology being developed to support the Department of the Air Force Data Fabric initiative. This ontology defines the semantic relationships between data assets, metadata standards, organizational data stewards, and mission threads. It integrates concepts from NIEM, Dublin Core, and DCAT to create a unified knowledge graph of DAF data resources, enabling intelligent data discovery and automated policy enforcement.

title: Linked Open Vocabularies (LOV)
description: Linked Open Vocabularies is an observatory and registry for ontologies and vocabularies published on the open web. LOV catalogs over 700 vocabularies with their relationships, usage statistics, and provenance. DoD and IC practitioners use LOV as a reference to discover existing vocabularies before creating new ones, ensuring alignment with community-accepted standards and reducing redundancy in ontology development.
